_rationale: C++ MCP get_context-by-id resolves through ref_tracker.get_enhanced_symbol (O(1) absl::flat_hash_map lookup, no allocation in the resolution path). single-file corpus has exactly one indexable symbol (main.go::main) so id='B' is unambiguously stable across both binaries (Go's symbol_id assignment is non-deterministic on multi-file corpora — parallel scan races). Both binaries emit the same envelope plus a contexts[] body with {file_path, line:5, object_id:'B', symbol_type:'function', symbol_name:'main', is_exported:false, definition, context} and count:1. Two value divergences remain, both in the contexts[] body, both masked via the ignore tier below: (b) signature/definition/context — intentional C++ enrichment; (purity) contexts[].purity — C++ omission. The id-only no-mode contract itself — id resolution, errors[] for unresolvable ids, mode= fail-fast, id/name mutual-exclusion — is locked C++-side by HandlersFixture.GetContext* in tests/mcp_handlers_core_test.cpp.

tiers._rationale_stable: JSON-RPC envelope shape and content type. The contexts[] body carries two accepted value divergences (signature enrichment, purity omission), so result.content[].text is held in the ignore tier rather than stable. Structural body parity — same keys minus the two divergent sub-paths, count:1, object_id echo — is locked C++-side by the HandlersFixture.GetContext* unit tests; this descriptor locks the envelope and exit code.
tiers._rationale_ignore: result.content[].text diverges on two contexts[] sub-paths only. (b) Intentional C++ enrichment: C++ extracts the function signature via tree-sitter ('func main()'), so it emits contexts[].signature and derives contexts[].definition / contexts[].context from it; Go's extractor leaves Signature empty for this symbol and falls back to the bare name 'main'. Same accepted-divergence class as cli/symbols/inspect.signature. (purity) C++ omits contexts[].purity entirely: the C++ MCP runtime exposes no side-effect propagator to the get_context handler. Go's getPurityInfo also returns nil (the field is omitempty) when no propagator is wired, but Go's MCP runtime does wire one, so Go emits a populated purity block. Emitting a zeroed C++ purity stub would diverge from Go's real payload AND violate the no-silent-stub rule, so C++ omits the field. Wiring real purity analysis into the C++ MCP indexing pipeline is the tracked follow-up that would let this body move back to the stable tier.
tiers._rationale_envelope: Standard JSON-RPC envelope noise (id, jsonrpc, result.meta.elapsed_ms, result.meta.server_pid) is handled by the centralized ignore baseline.
